description: IEEE 33-Bus State Estimates — Fault: 40-step fault/reconfiguration scenario estimator view. Panels: estimated vs. true bus voltage overlay during fault, per-bus error during fault, trace_P (ORACS observability index — expected to RISE during faulted_isolated as island-mode covariance inflates), NIS calibration (nis_k live series, EKF/UKF only), island-mode P-inflation (sigma_V / trace_P higher in faulted_isolated than pre_fault), and pre_fault/faulted_isolated/restored phase-region markers. The P-inflation panel is the ORACS island-resilience showpiece.

panels.description: Estimated bus voltage magnitudes (vm_pu_est) from the estimates bucket vs. true ground-truth vm_pu from the fault_event bucket across the 40-step fault scenario. Target A = estimated values; Target B = true state (oracle join allowed in dashboard — estimator code never reads fault_event). Key observation: bus 17 (μPMU) goes dark during faulted_isolated; the estimator must reconstruct it via Kirchhoff coupling through Ybus. The sigma_vm (1-sigma band) is visible when plotting bus 17 — covariance inflation in the dead zone is the island-mode resilience signal.

targets.query:
from(bucket: "estimates")
  |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._measurement == "estimate")
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._field == "vm_pu_est")
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r.estimator == "${estimator}")
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r.scenario  == "${scenario}")
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r.experiment == "fault")
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r.bus_id    == "${bus_id}")
  |> sort(columns: ["_time"])

from(bucket: "fault_event")
  |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._measurement == "bus")
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._field == "vm_pu")
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r.bus_id == "${bus_id}")
  |> sort(columns: ["_time"])

panels.title: True vs Estimated Bus Voltage (pu) — Fault Scenario, Bus ${bus_id}

panels.description: Island-mode P-inflation: trace(P) and mean sigma_V across the 40-step fault scenario. This is the ORACS island-resilience showpiece. During faulted_isolated, dead buses 8-17 have no real-sensor coverage, so the posterior covariance P inflates — trace(P) rises. During restored, tie-34 brings back topology coverage and trace(P) decreases toward the pre_fault level. The sigma_V = mean(sigma_vm) over all energised buses is plotted alongside trace_P as a per-bus uncertainty metric. The ratio trace_P(faulted_isolated) / trace_P(pre_fault) quantifies the ORACS observability degradation during island isolation. This connects directly to the AGMS 'covariance = observability index' talking point.

targets.query:
from(bucket: "estimates")
  |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._measurement == "estimate_system")
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._field == "trace_P")
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r.estimator  == "${estimator}")
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r.scenario   == "${scenario}")
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r.experiment == "fault")
  |> sort(columns: ["_time"])

from(bucket: "estimates")
  |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._measurement == "estimate")
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._field == "sigma_vm")
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r.estimator  == "${estimator}")
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r.scenario   == "${scenario}")
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r.experiment == "fault")
  |> group(columns: ["_time"])
  |> mean()
  |> group()
  |> sort(columns: ["_time"])

panels.title: Island-Mode P-Inflation: trace(P) and mean sigma_V (faulted_isolated rises)

panels.description: Phase-region marker: shows the current fault-scenario phase (pre_fault / faulted_isolated / restored) from the re-published event measurement in the measurements bucket. The n_dead_buses field follows the 0→10→0 pattern: 0 in pre_fault (13 steps), 10 during faulted_isolated (7 steps, buses 8-17 de-energised), 0 again in restored (20 steps). Colour mapping: 0 = green (normal), ≥1 = red (isolated/fault). This is the measurement-layer view of the event — three-block structure confirms phase transitions for the trace_P P-inflation panel above.

targets.query:
from(bucket: "measurements")
  |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._measurement == "event")
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._field == "n_dead_buses")
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r.experiment == "fault")
  |> sort(columns: ["_time"])

panels.title: Phase Marker — Peak Dead-Bus Count (0=normal, 10=isolated)

panels.description: Phase-region timeseries showing n_dead_buses over all 40 steps, from the measurements bucket event measurement. Shows the three-block event structure: pre_fault block (n_dead=0, 13 steps), faulted_isolated block (n_dead=10, 7 steps), restored block (n_dead=0, 20 steps). Compare this block structure to the trace_P panel above to confirm that covariance inflation aligns with the isolation window. The estimator's trace_P should track this block structure: rising in the faulted_isolated window and recovering in restored.

targets.query:
from(bucket: "measurements")
  |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._measurement == "event")
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._field == "n_dead_buses")
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r.experiment == "fault")
  |> sort(columns: ["_time"])

panels.title: n_dead_buses Over Time — pre_fault / faulted_isolated / restored Blocks

panels.description: NIS calibration panel for the fault scenario — the live nis_k time series persisted by the recursive filters (EKF/UKF) into the estimate_system measurement. During faulted_isolated, the innovation statistics change because measurements from dead buses vanish — m_k drops and nis_k may spike if the filter's prediction uncertainty is mis-calibrated for the island topology. During restored, m_k recovers as measurements resume. For WLS runs this series is EMPTY — NIS is a recursive-filter metric only.

targets.query:
from(bucket: "estimates")
  |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._measurement == "estimate_system")
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._field == "nis_k")
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r.estimator  == "${estimator}")
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r.scenario   == "${scenario}")
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r.experiment == "fault")
  |> sort(columns: ["_time"])

from(bucket: "estimates")
  |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._measurement == "estimate_system")
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._field == "m_k")
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r.estimator  == "${estimator}")
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r.scenario   == "${scenario}")
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r.experiment == "fault")
  |> sort(columns: ["_time"])

panels.title: NIS Calibration — Fault Scenario (nis_k live series, EKF/UKF only; empty for WLS)

panels.description: Per-bus estimation error |vm_pu_est - vm_pu| during the fault scenario. During faulted_isolated, buses 8-17 are in the dead zone — their error typically spikes as the estimator's prediction (FASE predict step) must carry the state without sensor updates. During restored, the error recovers. The σ_V uncertainty of buses 8-17 should be noticeably higher during isolation. This panel joins the estimates and fault_event buckets for error computation — a dashboard-level oracle join only.

targets.query:
import "math"

est = from(bucket: "estimates")
  |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._measurement == "estimate")
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._field == "vm_pu_est")
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r.estimator  == "${estimator}")
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r.scenario   == "${scenario}")
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r.experiment == "fault")
  |> rename(columns: {_value: "vm_pu_est"})

true_v = from(bucket: "fault_event")
  |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._measurement == "bus")
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._field == "vm_pu")
  |> rename(columns: {_value: "vm_pu"})

join(tables: {est: est, true_v: true_v}, on: ["_time", "bus_id"])
  |> map(fn: (r) => ({ r with _value: math.abs(x: r.vm_pu_est - r.vm_pu) }))
  |> group(columns: ["bus_id"])
  |> sort(columns: ["_time"])

panels.title: Per-Bus Estimation Error |vm_pu_est - vm_pu| — Fault (dead-zone buses spike during isolation)

title: IEEE33 Estimator Fault
